boundary_findings: POST /api/action accepts ui_action and rejects command_id payloads
allowed actions are refresh_health_detail, export_diagnostics, and stable_repair_plan
sync, stable_repair_apply, and launch_client are blocked
action panel updates dedicated DOM nodes and does not overwrite primary runtime truth
diagnostics is treated as action result, not runtime truth
stable repair dry-run is non-mutating by contract
no direct state/log/registry reads were introduced in contour files
initial_fail_reason: wild_boar_proxy/runtime.py is dirty in the working tree, so the auditor could not prove from a clean baseline that it was not changed by this contour.
remediation_required: record runtime.py as pre-existing dirty outside the contour
prove staged contour files exclude runtime.py, web_ui.py, and ui_shell.py
rerun independent audit after staged-only evidence is available
remediation_evidence: git diff --cached --name-only for runtime.py, web_ui.py, and ui_shell.py returned no paths
independent repeat audit verified the staged diff excludes runtime.py, web_ui.py, and ui_shell.py
independent repeat audit verified ui_action allowlist dispatch and command_id payload rejection
independent repeat audit verified only non-mutating actions are allowlisted
independent repeat audit verified action results render separately from primary live overview truth
independent repeat audit verified no direct state/log/runtime file access was introduced

findings: staged files still exclude runtime.py, web_ui.py, and ui_shell.py
live snapshot validation no longer requires fixture-only state_id
direct source=live and manual source switching are semantically honest in code
safe actions remain ui_action-only and separate from primary truth
no new direct state/log/runtime file access is present in the web-design files

residual_risks: runtime.py remains dirty outside this contour
runtime behavior of safe adapter commands still depends on their command packets staying non-mutating
new action tests validate wiring and blocking; real smoke is recorded separately
